category_title: Deaf and hard of hearing children with ASD who exhibit escape-motivated tantrums during difficult tasks
scenario_title: Difficult reading lesson triggering escape-motivated tantrum
minimal_context: Charlie is sitting at a table in his classroom during reading class, and the teacher presents him with a book to read a paragraph.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Charlie, it is time for reading class. Please look at this book and read the first paragraph.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Shakes his head and pushes the reading material away, refusing to work.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to finish this reading lesson.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Turns his body away from the table, continuing to show avoidance behaviors toward the difficult work assignment.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please turn around and read the paragraph.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Screams, pounds his fist on the table, and leaves his chair to avoid the reading work.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Charlie, it is time for reading class. Please look at this book and read the first paragraph.

STUDENT: (Shakes his head and pushes the reading material away, refusing to work.)

TUTOR: We need to finish this reading lesson.

STUDENT: (Turns his body away from the table, continuing to show avoidance behaviors toward the difficult work assignment.)

TUTOR: Please turn around and read the paragraph.

STUDENT: (Screams, pounds his fist on the table, and leaves his chair to avoid the reading work.)
